---
title: "Prediction"
description: "Predict stable candidate geometries across multiple relevant distances"
author: "Karel Dieussaert"
date: "2026-01-07"
categories: [Prediction]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---
# Prediction
This example demonstrates `aligner.predict(...)` for selecting meaningful candidate results from a distance series.

## What you learn

1. How to run prediction on a range of `relevant_distances`.
2. How to retrieve prediction results with `AlignerResultType.PREDICTIONS`.
3. How to visualize both geometry candidates and distance-vs-difference curves.

## When to use this

Use prediction when you do not want to manually inspect every processed distance and need a compact set of candidate outcomes.

## Notes

- This example loads GRB data (`GRBActualLoader`), so execution depends on available network connectivity.
- The map and chart are complementary:
  - map = spatial interpretation of candidates
  - chart = where geometric change stabilizes

```{python}
#| label: fig-plots
#| fig-cap:
#|   - "Graphic"
#|   - "Visual predictions"
{{< include ../../examples/example_predictor.py >}}
```
